# AgentPress v1 — Atomic Requirements Specification

**Source Documents:**
- `/home/agent/shipyard-ai/prds/prd-agentpress-2026-05-03.md`
- `/home/agent/shipyard-ai/rounds/prd-agentpress-2026-05-03/decisions.md`

**Note:** Decisions.md OVERRIDES PRD where they conflict.

---

## Plugin Bootstrap & Structure

### R-001: Plugin Main File Bootstrap
- **Description:** Main plugin file with standard WordPress plugin header and `AgentPress` namespace
- **Source:** PRD §5.1; Decisions File Structure
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - File `agentpress/agentpress.php` exists with valid WP plugin header (Plugin Name, Version, Author, etc.)
  - Namespace `AgentPress` is declared/used consistently
  - Plugin activates via WP admin or WP CLI without errors

### R-002: Activation Hook — Capability CPT
- **Description:** On activation, register private CPT `agentpress_capability`
- **Source:** PRD §5.1; Decision 7
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - CPT `agentpress_capability` is registered with `public => false`, `show_ui => false`
  - CPT is available for internal storage of capability definitions

### R-003: Activation Hook — Log CPT
- **Description:** On activation, register private CPT `agentpress_log` for task history
- **Source:** PRD §5.1; Decision 7
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - CPT `agentpress_log` is registered with `public => false`, `show_ui => false`
  - CPT is queryable by admin for log viewer

### R-004: File Structure Compliance
- **Description:** Plugin files match the locked file structure from decisions
- **Source:** Decisions File Structure (§File Structure)
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - Files exist: `agentpress.php`, `includes/class-router.php`, `includes/class-rest-api.php`, `includes/class-agents.php`, `includes/class-logger.php`, `includes/class-parser.php`, `includes/agents/class-content-writer.php`, `includes/agents/class-image-generator.php`, `admin/class-admin.php`, `admin/css/agentpress-admin.css`, `readme.txt`
  - No extra files beyond this structure (enforces "no bloat")

---

## REST API Endpoint

### R-005: REST Namespace Registration
- **Description:** Register REST namespace `agentpress/v1`
- **Source:** PRD §5.1; PRD §6
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - Endpoint accessible at `/wp-json/agentpress/v1/run`
  - Returns 404 for unregistered routes under `agentpress/v1`

### R-006: Single REST Endpoint
- **Description:** One endpoint `POST /wp-json/agentpress/v1/run` as primary interface
- **Source:** PRD §5.3; Decision 5; Decision 9
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - Endpoint accepts POST requests only
  - No other REST endpoints exist in v1 (no manual runner, no chat, no developer API)

### R-007: Authentication — Nonce Support
- **Description:** Accept `X-WP-Nonce` header from logged-in users
- **Source:** PRD §5.3
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - Request with valid `X-WP-Nonce` from authenticated WP user is accepted
  - Invalid/missing nonce returns 401/403

### R-008: Authentication — Application Passwords
- **Description:** Accept WordPress Application Passwords for authentication
- **Source:** PRD §5.3
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - Basic Auth with Application Password credentials is accepted
  - Invalid credentials return 401

### R-009: Authorization Capability Check
- **Description:** Require `manage_options` or custom `agentpress_run` capability
- **Source:** PRD §5.3
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - Users without required capability receive 403 Forbidden
  - Users with `manage_options` can access endpoint

### R-010: Request Body Schema Validation
- **Description:** Validate request body contains required `task` string and optional `context`
- **Source:** PRD §5.3; PRD §6
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - Missing `task` returns 400 with `agentpress_missing_task` code
  - `context` is optional; defaults to empty object if omitted
  - `context` may contain `post_id` (int) and `site_name` (string)

### R-011: Success Response Shape
- **Description:** Return standardized 200 response with success, routing, result, latency_ms
- **Source:** PRD §5.3; PRD §6
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - Response JSON contains: `success` (bool), `routing` (object with `capability`, `reasoning`), `result` (object), `latency_ms` (int)
  - `routing.capability` matches executed agent slug
  - `latency_ms` reflects end-to-end execution time

### R-012: Error Response Shape
- **Description:** Return standardized WP-REST error format for failures
- **Source:** PRD §6
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - 400 errors include `code`, `message`, `data.status`
  - 500 errors include `code` (`agentpress_agent_error`), `message`, `data.status`, `data.capability`

### R-013: No Real-Time Streaming
- **Description:** Synchronous REST response only; no streaming
- **Source:** PRD §3 (Non-Goals); Decision 11
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - Endpoint blocks until task completes or times out
  - No `text/event-stream` or chunked transfer encoding
  - Timeout configured at 30 seconds

---

## Router & Routing Logic

### R-014: Local PHP Keyword Map
- **Description:** Router uses local PHP keyword matching for built-in agents
- **Source:** Decision 2
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - `str_contains` or equivalent PHP matching for keyword detection
  - Keywords trigger direct agent routing without Claude call
  - Keyword map is defined/configurable in code

### R-015: Claude Fallback for Ambiguity
- **Description:** Claude routing used only when local keyword map fails to match
- **Source:** Decision 2
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - If keyword match succeeds, Claude is NOT called
  - If no keyword match, Claude API is called for routing
  - Fallback behavior is testable/verifiable

### R-016: Keyword Whitelist Definition
- **Description:** Locked whitelist of keywords for ContentWriter vs ImageGenerator
- **Source:** Decisions Open Questions #2
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - Whitelist documented in code (e.g., `class-router.php`)
  - Image-related keywords (`image`, `picture`, `photo`, `graphic`) → `image_generator`
  - Content/writing keywords (`write`, `content`, `blog`, `post`, `article`, `text`) → `content_writer`

### R-017: Claude System Prompt Construction
- **Description:** When Claude fallback is used, build system prompt with capability manifest
- **Source:** PRD §5.3
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - System prompt includes available capabilities with descriptions
  - Prompt instructs Claude to return ONLY JSON (no markdown)
  - Prompt specifies JSON schema: `{"capability": "...", "reasoning": "...", "payload": {...}}`

### R-018: No Third-Party Routing in v1
- **Description:** Router only handles built-in agents; no external capability routing
- **Source:** Decision 8
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - Router does not call `agentpress_register_capability()` API
  - No mechanism for third-party agent registration exists in v1

---

## JSON Parser Hardening (Non-Negotiable)

### R-019: Markdown Fence Stripping
- **Description:** Parser strips markdown code fences from Claude responses
- **Source:** Decision 12; Decisions Risk Register
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - Responses wrapped in \`\`\`json...\`\`\` are parsed correctly
  - Fence markers are removed before JSON decode
  - Test case: \`\`\`json{"capability":"x"}\`\`\` → valid JSON

### R-020: Truncated Response Handling
- **Description:** Parser handles truncated/incomplete JSON responses
- **Source:** Decision 12; Decisions Risk Register
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - Partial JSON does not cause fatal error
  - Returns structured error with `agentpress_parser_error` code
  - Graceful degradation documented

### R-021: Hallucinated Slug Handling
- **Description:** Parser validates capability slug against known agents
- **Source:** Decision 12; Decisions Risk Register
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - Unknown slug returns error (not crash)
  - Error message indicates invalid capability
  - Fallback behavior: return `capability: none`

### R-022: JSON Schema Validation
- **Description:** Validate parsed JSON against expected schema
- **Source:** Decisions Open Questions #7
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - Required fields (`capability`) are validated
  - Missing required fields return parser error
  - Invalid field types are rejected

### R-023: Parser Budget — 30 Minutes Minimum
- **Description:** Parser hardening receives minimum 30 minutes of build time
- **Source:** Decision 12
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - Parser class (`class-parser.php`) exists with dedicated hardening logic
  - Parser has unit/integration test coverage for edge cases

---

## Built-In Agents

### R-024: ContentWriter Agent Registration
- **Description:** ContentWriter agent (`content_writer`) is registered and available
- **Source:** PRD §5.4; Decision 4
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - Agent slug is `content_writer`
  - Agent is callable via router
  - Agent appears in internal capability list

### R-025: ContentWriter Input Schema
- **Description:** ContentWriter accepts `topic`, `tone`, `length` parameters
- **Source:** PRD §5.4
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - `topic` (string): subject matter for content
  - `tone` (string, enum: `friendly`|`professional`|`witty`)
  - `length` (string, enum: `short`|`medium`|`long`)
  - Schema is enforced; invalid values return error

### R-026: ContentWriter Claude Integration
- **Description:** ContentWriter calls Claude API via `wp_remote_post`
- **Source:** PRD §5.4
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - Uses configured `claude_api_key` from settings
  - Calls Anthropic Messages API endpoint
  - Handles API errors gracefully

### R-027: ContentWriter Output Guardrail
- **Description:** ContentWriter output truncated to 2048 characters max
- **Source:** PRD §5.4
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - Output exceeding 2048 chars is truncated
  - Truncation does not cause errors
  - `tokens_used` is still reported

### R-028: ContentWriter Output Shape
- **Description:** ContentWriter returns `{"text": "...", "tokens_used": N}`
- **Source:** PRD §5.4
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - Response contains `text` (string) and `tokens_used` (int)
  - Empty/missing text returns error

### R-029: ImageGenerator Agent Registration
- **Description:** ImageGenerator agent (`image_generator`) is registered and available
- **Source:** PRD §5.4; Decision 4
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - Agent slug is `image_generator`
  - Agent is callable via router
  - Agent appears in internal capability list

### R-030: ImageGenerator Input Schema
- **Description:** ImageGenerator accepts `prompt`, `size` parameters
- **Source:** PRD §5.4
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - `prompt` (string): description of image to generate
  - `size` (string, enum: `512x512`|`1024x1024`)
  - Schema is enforced; invalid values return error

### R-031: ImageGenerator Cloudflare Workers AI Integration
- **Description:** ImageGenerator calls Cloudflare Workers AI (Flux/Stable Diffusion)
- **Source:** PRD §5.4
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - Uses configured `cf_worker_url` from settings
  - Calls Worker endpoint via `wp_remote_post`
  - Handles Worker API errors gracefully

### R-032: ImageGenerator HTTPS Guardrail
- **Description:** Generated image URL must be HTTPS
- **Source:** PRD §5.4
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - HTTP URLs are rejected/converted
  - Non-HTTPS response returns error

### R-033: ImageGenerator Output Shape
- **Description:** ImageGenerator returns `{"url": "...", "format": "...", "size": "..."}`
- **Source:** PRD §5.4
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - Response contains `url` (HTTPS string), `format` (e.g., `webp`), `size` (e.g., `1024x1024`)
  - Missing URL returns error

### R-034: ImageGenerator Error Handling
- **Description:** Worker response failure returns WP_Error shape
- **Source:** PRD §5.4
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - Failed Worker call returns structured error
  - Error includes original Worker error message

### R-035: SEOMeta Agent Excluded
- **Description:** SEOMeta agent is NOT included in v1
- **Source:** Decision 4
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - No `class-seo-meta.php` or similar file exists
  - No `seo_meta` capability is registered
  - Requests for SEO meta return error or route to none

### R-036: Third Agent Slot Reserved
- **Description:** Third agent slot exists in file structure but ships empty
- **Source:** Decision 4; Decisions File Structure
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - File `includes/agents/class-agent-third.php` exists as placeholder
  - File contains minimal stub (no functional code)
  - Third agent is NOT registered/active in v1

---

## Capability Registry

### R-037: CPT-Based Capability Storage
- **Description:** Capabilities stored as `agentpress_capability` CPT with post_meta
- **Source:** PRD §5.2; Decision 7
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - Capability data stored in post meta: `_agentpress_name`, `_agentpress_description`, `_agentpress_input_schema`, `_agentpress_handler`
  - `post_name` = capability slug
  - `post_title` = human name

### R-038: Built-In Agents Auto-Registered
- **Description:** ContentWriter and ImageGenerator registered on activation
- **Source:** PRD §5.2 (implied); Decision 4
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - On plugin activation, both built-in agents are inserted into CPT
  - Agents are queryable immediately after activation

### R-039: No Third-Party Registration API
- **Description:** `agentpress_register_capability()` function does NOT exist in v1
- **Source:** Decision 8
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - Function `agentpress_register_capability()` is not defined
  - No mechanism for external plugins to register capabilities
  - README does not document third-party registration

---

## Activity Logging

### R-040: Log Every Task to CPT
- **Description:** Every task execution writes to `agentpress_log` CPT
- **Source:** PRD §5.3; Decision 7
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - Log entry created on task completion (success or error)
  - `post_title` = truncated task string
  - Log is queryable by admin

### R-041: Log Meta Fields
- **Description:** Log entries store: capability, payload, result, latency_ms, status, timestamp
- **Source:** PRD §5.3; PRD §7; Decisions Open Questions #4
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - Meta keys: `_agentpress_capability`, `_agentpress_payload`, `_agentpress_result`, `_agentpress_latency_ms`, `_agentpress_status`
  - `status` is `success` or `error`
  - `timestamp` stored as post_date

### R-042: Auto-Prune Policy
- **Description:** Auto-prune logs when count exceeds 500; delete oldest 50
- **Source:** PRD §7; Decisions Open Questions #4
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - On log insert, if count > 500, oldest 50 logs are deleted
  - Pruning does not block task execution
  - Log count never exceeds 550

### R-043: Log Viewer in Admin
- **Description:** Activity log visible in wp-admin dashboard
- **Source:** Decision 7; PRD §5.5
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - Admin screen displays last 50 logs
  - Each row shows: time, capability, status (colored pill), latency
  - Status pills: green `success`, red `error`

---

## Admin Dashboard

### R-044: Single Settings Screen
- **Description:** One admin page under Tools menu; no tabs
- **Source:** Decision 3; Decision 5; PRD §5.5
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - Page slug: `tools-agentpress`
  - Menu location: Tools submenu
  - No tab navigation exists
  - No wizards or multi-step flows

### R-045: No Manual Task Runner
- **Description:** No textarea form for manual task execution
- **Source:** Decision 5
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - Admin screen does NOT contain task input textarea
  - No "Run" button for ad-hoc task execution
  - Testing done via cURL/Postman only

### R-046: API Key Configuration Field
- **Description:** Text input for Claude API key
- **Source:** PRD §5.5; Decision 6
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - Field labeled "Claude API Key" or similar
  - Value saved to `agentpress_settings` option
  - Field is type="password" for input masking

### R-047: Cloudflare Worker URL Field
- **Description:** Text input for Cloudflare Worker URL
- **Source:** PRD §5.5
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - Field labeled for Worker URL
  - Value saved to `agentpress_settings` option
  - Validation ensures URL format

### R-048: Default Model Selection
- **Description:** Select dropdown for Claude model
- **Source:** PRD §5.5
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - Default option: `claude-3-5-sonnet-20241022`
  - Value saved to `agentpress_settings` option

### R-049: Standard (Unencrypted) Key Storage
- **Description:** API keys stored as plain options or wp-config constants
- **Source:** Decision 6
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - No `wp_hash()` or `base64_encode()` encryption applied
  - Keys stored in `agentpress_settings` option as plain text
  - Option supports wp-config.php constant override (e.g., `AGENTPRESS_CLAUDE_KEY`)

### R-050: Native WordPress Styling
- **Description:** Admin uses core WP styles; minimal custom CSS
- **Source:** PRD §5.5; PRD §8; Decision 3
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - Uses `.wp-list-table`, `.form-table` core classes
  - No CSS frameworks (Tailwind, Bootstrap)
  - Custom CSS file exists but is minimal (spacing, typography only)
  - Colors use WP admin defaults (`#2271b1`, etc.)

### R-051: Activity Log Display
- **Description:** Log viewer shows last 50 tasks with status indicators
- **Source:** PRD §5.5; Decision 3
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - Table shows: timestamp, capability name, status pill, latency
  - Status pills: green for success, red for error
  - Most recent entries first

### R-052: No Front-End Chat UI
- **Description:** No chat widget or front-end interface
- **Source:** Decision 9; PRD §3 (Non-Goals)
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - No shortcode exists for front-end chat
  - No public-facing UI components
  - All interaction via REST API or admin

### R-053: No Onboarding Wizard
- **Description:** No setup wizard or multi-step onboarding
- **Source:** PRD §8; Decision 5
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - Admin screen is immediate; no "Get Started" flow
  - Setup instructions in README only

---

## Settings & Storage

### R-054: Settings Option Structure
- **Description:** All settings stored in `agentpress_settings` serialized option
- **Source:** PRD §7
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - Option key: `agentpress_settings`
  - Contains: `claude_api_key`, `cf_worker_url`, `default_model`, `installed_version`
  - Serialized as PHP array

### R-055: No Encrypted Storage
- **Description:** API keys NOT encrypted (security theater removed)
- **Source:** Decision 6
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - No encryption/decryption functions in codebase
  - Keys readable in database as plain text
  - README discloses plain-text storage

### R-056: wp-config.php Constant Support
- **Description:** Settings can be overridden via wp-config.php constants
- **Source:** Decision 6
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - Constants checked before option values (e.g., `AGENTPRESS_CLAUDE_KEY`)
  - If constant defined, UI field is disabled/hidden
  - Constants take precedence over saved options

---

## Distribution & Documentation

### R-057: WordPress.org Readme
- **Description:** `readme.txt` with three paragraphs; demo-focused
- **Source:** PRD §9; Decision 10
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - File `readme.txt` exists with WP.org header format
  - Description is ~3 paragraphs
  - Tags include: `ai`, `agents`, `automation`, `claude`, `orchestration`
  - API usage disclosed (per WordPress.org TOS)

### R-058: README Installation Instructions
- **Description:** README includes installation and cURL API example
- **Source:** PRD §9
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - Installation steps documented
  - cURL example for `/wp-json/agentpress/v1/run`
  - No third-party registration example (v2 feature)

### R-059: Plugin Assets for Repo
- **Description:** Banner (772×250) and screenshots captured
- **Source:** PRD §10 (Dogfooding)
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - Banner image exists at 772×250
  - At least one screenshot of admin dashboard
  - Assets ready for WP.org submission

### R-060: No Billing/SaaS Mentions
- **Description:** No "Pro", billing, or SaaS tier references in v1
- **Source:** Decision 8; PRD §3 (Non-Goals)
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - Codebase contains no "Pro", "Premium", "tier" references
  - README does not mention monetization
  - No upgrade prompts in admin UI

---

## Non-Functional Requirements

### R-061: No Async Queues
- **Description:** All processing is synchronous; no background queues
- **Source:** Decision 11; PRD §3 (Non-Goals)
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - No WP-Cron integration for task processing
  - No queue library or abstraction
  - All tasks complete within single request

### R-062: No BYOK Model
- **Description:** Single site-level API key; no per-user keys
- **Source:** Decision 11; PRD §14 (Open Questions)
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - Only one Claude API key configured per site
  - No user-level key assignment UI
  - BYOK noted as v2 trigger (500 daily tasks)

### R-063: 30-Second Timeout
- **Description:** REST endpoint times out at 30 seconds
- **Source:** PRD §14 (Open Questions)
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - `wp_remote_post` timeout set to 30s
  - Timeout returns structured error (not PHP fatal)
  - Error indicates timeout condition

### R-064: Latency Target < 4 Seconds
- **Description:** Average end-to-end latency under 4 seconds for built-in agents
- **Source:** PRD §13 (Success Metrics)
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - `latency_ms` in response averages < 4000ms
  - Measured over 100+ tasks
  - Local keyword routing should improve baseline

### R-065: Zero Fatal Errors
- **Description:** No fatal PHP errors under normal operation
- **Source:** PRD §13 (Success Metrics)
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - `wp_debug.log` shows no fatal errors after 100 tasks
  - All errors handled gracefully with WP_Error or REST error response

---

## Exclusions (Explicitly NOT in v1)

### R-066: Exclusion — SEOMeta Agent
- **Description:** SEOMeta agent is dead; does not ship
- **Source:** Decision 4
- **Acceptance Criteria:** No SEOMeta code, routes, or references

### R-067: Exclusion — Manual Task Runner UI
- **Description:** No textarea + Run button in admin
- **Source:** Decision 5
- **Acceptance Criteria:** No task input form in admin

### R-068: Exclusion — Developer API
- **Description:** No `agentpress_register_capability()` function
- **Source:** Decision 8
- **Acceptance Criteria:** No third-party registration mechanism

### R-069: Exclusion — Real-Time Streaming
- **Description:** No SSE, WebSocket, or chunked responses
- **Source:** Decision 11
- **Acceptance Criteria:** All responses are complete JSON

### R-070: Exclusion — Async Queues
- **Description:** No background job processing
- **Source:** Decision 11
- **Acceptance Criteria:** All tasks synchronous

### R-071: Exclusion — BYOK
- **Description:** No bring-your-own-key model
- **Source:** Decision 11
- **Acceptance Criteria:** Single site-level key only

### R-072: Exclusion — Front-End Chat
- **Description:** No chat widget or public UI
- **Source:** Decision 9
- **Acceptance Criteria:** REST API is only interface

### R-073: Exclusion — Onboarding Wizard
- **Description:** No setup wizard
- **Source:** Decision 5; PRD §8
- **Acceptance Criteria:** Single admin screen only

### R-074: Exclusion — Encrypted Key Storage
- **Description:** No wp_hash/base64 encryption
- **Source:** Decision 6
- **Acceptance Criteria:** Keys stored plain

### R-075: Exclusion — Third Agent (Functional)
- **Description:** Third agent slot reserved but not functional
- **Source:** Decision 4
- **Acceptance Criteria:** Placeholder file only; no registration

---

## Summary Count

| Category | Requirements |
|----------|-------------|
| Plugin Bootstrap & Structure | 4 |
| REST API Endpoint | 9 |
| Router & Routing Logic | 5 |
| JSON Parser Hardening | 5 |
| Built-In Agents | 13 |
| Capability Registry | 3 |
| Activity Logging | 4 |
| Admin Dashboard | 10 |
| Settings & Storage | 3 |
| Distribution & Documentation | 4 |
| Non-Functional | 5 |
| Exclusions | 10 |
| **Total** | **75** |

---

**Verification Note:** Each requirement above is atomic and independently verifiable. Build should proceed by checking off requirements as implemented. Any deviation from this spec requires decision log amendment.

- Only 2 agents ship: ContentWriter and ImageGenerator (Decision 4)
- No developer API / third-party registration hooks in v1 (Decision 8)
- No real-time streaming, no async queues, no BYOK (Decision 11)

content.text:
# AgentPress v1 — Atomic Requirements Specification

**Source Documents:**
- `/home/agent/shipyard-ai/prds/prd-agentpress-2026-05-03.md`
- `/home/agent/shipyard-ai/rounds/prd-agentpress-2026-05-03/decisions.md`

**Note:** Decisions.md OVERRIDES PRD where they conflict.

---

## Plugin Bootstrap & Structure

### R-001: Plugin Main File Bootstrap
- **Description:** Main plugin file with standard WordPress plugin header and `AgentPress` namespace
- **Source:** PRD §5.1; Decisions File Structure
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - File `agentpress/agentpress.php` exists with valid WP plugin header (Plugin Name, Version, Author, etc.)
  - Namespace `AgentPress` is declared/used consistently
  - Plugin activates via WP admin or WP CLI without errors

### R-002: Activation Hook — Capability CPT
- **Description:** On activation, register private CPT `agentpress_capability`
- **Source:** PRD §5.1; Decision 7
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - CPT `agentpress_capability` is registered with `public => false`, `show_ui => false`
  - CPT is available for internal storage of capability definitions

### R-003: Activation Hook — Log CPT
- **Description:** On activation, register private CPT `agentpress_log` for task history
- **Source:** PRD §5.1; Decision 7
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - CPT `agentpress_log` is registered with `public => false`, `show_ui => false`
  - CPT is queryable by admin for log viewer

### R-004: File Structure Compliance
- **Description:** Plugin files match the locked file structure from decisions
- **Source:** Decisions File Structure (§File Structure)
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - Files exist: `agentpress.php`, `includes/class-router.php`, `includes/class-rest-api.php`, `includes/class-agents.php`, `includes/class-logger.php`, `includes/class-parser.php`, `includes/agents/class-content-writer.php`, `includes/agents/class-image-generator.php`, `admin/class-admin.php`, `admin/css/agentpress-admin.css`, `readme.txt`
  - No extra files beyond this structure (enforces "no bloat")

---

## REST API Endpoint

### R-005: REST Namespace Registration
- **Description:** Register REST namespace `agentpress/v1`
- **Source:** PRD §5.1; PRD §6
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - Endpoint accessible at `/wp-json/agentpress/v1/run`
  - Returns 404 for unregistered routes under `agentpress/v1`

### R-006: Single REST Endpoint
- **Description:** One endpoint `POST /wp-json/agentpress/v1/run` as primary interface
- **Source:** PRD §5.3; Decision 5; Decision 9
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - Endpoint accepts POST requests only
  - No other REST endpoints exist in v1 (no manual runner, no chat, no developer API)

### R-007: Authentication — Nonce Support
- **Description:** Accept `X-WP-Nonce` header from logged-in users
- **Source:** PRD §5.3
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - Request with valid `X-WP-Nonce` from authenticated WP user is accepted
  - Invalid/missing nonce returns 401/403

### R-008: Authentication — Application Passwords
- **Description:** Accept WordPress Application Passwords for authentication
- **Source:** PRD §5.3
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - Basic Auth with Application Password credentials is accepted
  - Invalid credentials return 401

### R-009: Authorization Capability Check
- **Description:** Require `manage_options` or custom `agentpress_run` capability
- **Source:** PRD §5.3
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - Users without required capability receive 403 Forbidden
  - Users with `manage_options` can access endpoint

### R-010: Request Body Schema Validation
- **Description:** Validate request body contains required `task` string and optional `context`
- **Source:** PRD §5.3; PRD §6
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - Missing `task` returns 400 with `agentpress_missing_task` code
  - `context` is optional; defaults to empty object if omitted
  - `context` may contain `post_id` (int) and `site_name` (string)

### R-011: Success Response Shape
- **Description:** Return standardized 200 response with success, routing, result, latency_ms
- **Source:** PRD §5.3; PRD §6
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - Response JSON contains: `success` (bool), `routing` (object with `capability`, `reasoning`), `result` (object), `latency_ms` (int)
  - `routing.capability` matches executed agent slug
  - `latency_ms` reflects end-to-end execution time

### R-012: Error Response Shape
- **Description:** Return standardized WP-REST error format for failures
- **Source:** PRD §6
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - 400 errors include `code`, `message`, `data.status`
  - 500 errors include `code` (`agentpress_agent_error`), `message`, `data.status`, `data.capability`

### R-013: No Real-Time Streaming
- **Description:** Synchronous REST response only; no streaming
- **Source:** PRD §3 (Non-Goals); Decision 11
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - Endpoint blocks until task completes or times out
  - No `text/event-stream` or chunked transfer encoding
  - Timeout configured at 30 seconds

---

## Router & Routing Logic

### R-014: Local PHP Keyword Map
- **Description:** Router uses local PHP keyword matching for built-in agents
- **Source:** Decision 2
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - `str_contains` or equivalent PHP matching for keyword detection
  - Keywords trigger direct agent routing without Claude call
  - Keyword map is defined/configurable in code

### R-015: Claude Fallback for Ambiguity
- **Description:** Claude routing used only when local keyword map fails to match
- **Source:** Decision 2
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - If keyword match succeeds, Claude is NOT called
  - If no keyword match, Claude API is called for routing
  - Fallback behavior is testable/verifiable

### R-016: Keyword Whitelist Definition
- **Description:** Locked whitelist of keywords for ContentWriter vs ImageGenerator
- **Source:** Decisions Open Questions #2
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - Whitelist documented in code (e.g., `class-router.php`)
  - Image-related keywords (`image`, `picture`, `photo`, `graphic`) → `image_generator`
  - Content/writing keywords (`write`, `content`, `blog`, `post`, `article`, `text`) → `content_writer`

### R-017: Claude System Prompt Construction
- **Description:** When Claude fallback is used, build system prompt with capability manifest
- **Source:** PRD §5.3
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - System prompt includes available capabilities with descriptions
  - Prompt instructs Claude to return ONLY JSON (no markdown)
  - Prompt specifies JSON schema: `{"capability": "...", "reasoning": "...", "payload": {...}}`

### R-018: No Third-Party Routing in v1
- **Description:** Router only handles built-in agents; no external capability routing
- **Source:** Decision 8
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - Router does not call `agentpress_register_capability()` API
  - No mechanism for third-party agent registration exists in v1

---

## JSON Parser Hardening (Non-Negotiable)

### R-019: Markdown Fence Stripping
- **Description:** Parser strips markdown code fences from Claude responses
- **Source:** Decision 12; Decisions Risk Register
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - Responses wrapped in \`\`\`json...\`\`\` are parsed correctly
  - Fence markers are removed before JSON decode
  - Test case: \`\`\`json{"capability":"x"}\`\`\` → valid JSON

### R-020: Truncated Response Handling
- **Description:** Parser handles truncated/incomplete JSON responses
- **Source:** Decision 12; Decisions Risk Register
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - Partial JSON does not cause fatal error
  - Returns structured error with `agentpress_parser_error` code
  - Graceful degradation documented

### R-021: Hallucinated Slug Handling
- **Description:** Parser validates capability slug against known agents
- **Source:** Decision 12; Decisions Risk Register
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - Unknown slug returns error (not crash)
  - Error message indicates invalid capability
  - Fallback behavior: return `capability: none`

### R-022: JSON Schema Validation
- **Description:** Validate parsed JSON against expected schema
- **Source:** Decisions Open Questions #7
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - Required fields (`capability`) are validated
  - Missing required fields return parser error
  - Invalid field types are rejected

### R-023: Parser Budget — 30 Minutes Minimum
- **Description:** Parser hardening receives minimum 30 minutes of build time
- **Source:** Decision 12
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - Parser class (`class-parser.php`) exists with dedicated hardening logic
  - Parser has unit/integration test coverage for edge cases

---

## Built-In Agents

### R-024: ContentWriter Agent Registration
- **Description:** ContentWriter agent (`content_writer`) is registered and available
- **Source:** PRD §5.4; Decision 4
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - Agent slug is `content_writer`
  - Agent is callable via router
  - Agent appears in internal capability list

### R-025: ContentWriter Input Schema
- **Description:** ContentWriter accepts `topic`, `tone`, `length` parameters
- **Source:** PRD §5.4
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - `topic` (string): subject matter for content
  - `tone` (string, enum: `friendly`|`professional`|`witty`)
  - `length` (string, enum: `short`|`medium`|`long`)
  - Schema is enforced; invalid values return error

### R-026: ContentWriter Claude Integration
- **Description:** ContentWriter calls Claude API via `wp_remote_post`
- **Source:** PRD §5.4
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - Uses configured `claude_api_key` from settings
  - Calls Anthropic Messages API endpoint
  - Handles API errors gracefully

### R-027: ContentWriter Output Guardrail
- **Description:** ContentWriter output truncated to 2048 characters max
- **Source:** PRD §5.4
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - Output exceeding 2048 chars is truncated
  - Truncation does not cause errors
  - `tokens_used` is still reported

### R-028: ContentWriter Output Shape
- **Description:** ContentWriter returns `{"text": "...", "tokens_used": N}`
- **Source:** PRD §5.4
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - Response contains `text` (string) and `tokens_used` (int)
  - Empty/missing text returns error

### R-029: ImageGenerator Agent Registration
- **Description:** ImageGenerator agent (`image_generator`) is registered and available
- **Source:** PRD §5.4; Decision 4
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - Agent slug is `image_generator`
  - Agent is callable via router
  - Agent appears in internal capability list

### R-030: ImageGenerator Input Schema
- **Description:** ImageGenerator accepts `prompt`, `size` parameters
- **Source:** PRD §5.4
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - `prompt` (string): description of image to generate
  - `size` (string, enum: `512x512`|`1024x1024`)
  - Schema is enforced; invalid values return error

### R-031: ImageGenerator Cloudflare Workers AI Integration
- **Description:** ImageGenerator calls Cloudflare Workers AI (Flux/Stable Diffusion)
- **Source:** PRD §5.4
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - Uses configured `cf_worker_url` from settings
  - Calls Worker endpoint via `wp_remote_post`
  - Handles Worker API errors gracefully

### R-032: ImageGenerator HTTPS Guardrail
- **Description:** Generated image URL must be HTTPS
- **Source:** PRD §5.4
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - HTTP URLs are rejected/converted
  - Non-HTTPS response returns error

### R-033: ImageGenerator Output Shape
- **Description:** ImageGenerator returns `{"url": "...", "format": "...", "size": "..."}`
- **Source:** PRD §5.4
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - Response contains `url` (HTTPS string), `format` (e.g., `webp`), `size` (e.g., `1024x1024`)
  - Missing URL returns error

### R-034: ImageGenerator Error Handling
- **Description:** Worker response failure returns WP_Error shape
- **Source:** PRD §5.4
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - Failed Worker call returns structured error
  - Error includes original Worker error message

### R-035: SEOMeta Agent Excluded
- **Description:** SEOMeta agent is NOT included in v1
- **Source:** Decision 4
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - No `class-seo-meta.php` or similar file exists
  - No `seo_meta` capability is registered
  - Requests for SEO meta return error or route to none

### R-036: Third Agent Slot Reserved
- **Description:** Third agent slot exists in file structure but ships empty
- **Source:** Decision 4; Decisions File Structure
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - File `includes/agents/class-agent-third.php` exists as placeholder
  - File contains minimal stub (no functional code)
  - Third agent is NOT registered/active in v1

---

## Capability Registry

### R-037: CPT-Based Capability Storage
- **Description:** Capabilities stored as `agentpress_capability` CPT with post_meta
- **Source:** PRD §5.2; Decision 7
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - Capability data stored in post meta: `_agentpress_name`, `_agentpress_description`, `_agentpress_input_schema`, `_agentpress_handler`
  - `post_name` = capability slug
  - `post_title` = human name

### R-038: Built-In Agents Auto-Registered
- **Description:** ContentWriter and ImageGenerator registered on activation
- **Source:** PRD §5.2 (implied); Decision 4
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - On plugin activation, both built-in agents are inserted into CPT
  - Agents are queryable immediately after activation

### R-039: No Third-Party Registration API
- **Description:** `agentpress_register_capability()` function does NOT exist in v1
- **Source:** Decision 8
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - Function `agentpress_register_capability()` is not defined
  - No mechanism for external plugins to register capabilities
  - README does not document third-party registration

---

## Activity Logging

### R-040: Log Every Task to CPT
- **Description:** Every task execution writes to `agentpress_log` CPT
- **Source:** PRD §5.3; Decision 7
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - Log entry created on task completion (success or error)
  - `post_title` = truncated task string
  - Log is queryable by admin

### R-041: Log Meta Fields
- **Description:** Log entries store: capability, payload, result, latency_ms, status, timestamp
- **Source:** PRD §5.3; PRD §7; Decisions Open Questions #4
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - Meta keys: `_agentpress_capability`, `_agentpress_payload`, `_agentpress_result`, `_agentpress_latency_ms`, `_agentpress_status`
  - `status` is `success` or `error`
  - `timestamp` stored as post_date

### R-042: Auto-Prune Policy
- **Description:** Auto-prune logs when count exceeds 500; delete oldest 50
- **Source:** PRD §7; Decisions Open Questions #4
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - On log insert, if count > 500, oldest 50 logs are deleted
  - Pruning does not block task execution
  - Log count never exceeds 550

### R-043: Log Viewer in Admin
- **Description:** Activity log visible in wp-admin dashboard
- **Source:** Decision 7; PRD §5.5
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - Admin screen displays last 50 logs
  - Each row shows: time, capability, status (colored pill), latency
  - Status pills: green `success`, red `error`

---

## Admin Dashboard

### R-044: Single Settings Screen
- **Description:** One admin page under Tools menu; no tabs
- **Source:** Decision 3; Decision 5; PRD §5.5
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - Page slug: `tools-agentpress`
  - Menu location: Tools submenu
  - No tab navigation exists
  - No wizards or multi-step flows

### R-045: No Manual Task Runner
- **Description:** No textarea form for manual task execution
- **Source:** Decision 5
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - Admin screen does NOT contain task input textarea
  - No "Run" button for ad-hoc task execution
  - Testing done via cURL/Postman only

### R-046: API Key Configuration Field
- **Description:** Text input for Claude API key
- **Source:** PRD §5.5; Decision 6
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - Field labeled "Claude API Key" or similar
  - Value saved to `agentpress_settings` option
  - Field is type="password" for input masking

### R-047: Cloudflare Worker URL Field
- **Description:** Text input for Cloudflare Worker URL
- **Source:** PRD §5.5
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - Field labeled for Worker URL
  - Value saved to `agentpress_settings` option
  - Validation ensures URL format

### R-048: Default Model Selection
- **Description:** Select dropdown for Claude model
- **Source:** PRD §5.5
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - Default option: `claude-3-5-sonnet-20241022`
  - Value saved to `agentpress_settings` option

### R-049: Standard (Unencrypted) Key Storage
- **Description:** API keys stored as plain options or wp-config constants
- **Source:** Decision 6
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - No `wp_hash()` or `base64_encode()` encryption applied
  - Keys stored in `agentpress_settings` option as plain text
  - Option supports wp-config.php constant override (e.g., `AGENTPRESS_CLAUDE_KEY`)

### R-050: Native WordPress Styling
- **Description:** Admin uses core WP styles; minimal custom CSS
- **Source:** PRD §5.5; PRD §8; Decision 3
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - Uses `.wp-list-table`, `.form-table` core classes
  - No CSS frameworks (Tailwind, Bootstrap)
  - Custom CSS file exists but is minimal (spacing, typography only)
  - Colors use WP admin defaults (`#2271b1`, etc.)

### R-051: Activity Log Display
- **Description:** Log viewer shows last 50 tasks with status indicators
- **Source:** PRD §5.5; Decision 3
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - Table shows: timestamp, capability name, status pill, latency
  - Status pills: green for success, red for error
  - Most recent entries first

### R-052: No Front-End Chat UI
- **Description:** No chat widget or front-end interface
- **Source:** Decision 9; PRD §3 (Non-Goals)
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - No shortcode exists for front-end chat
  - No public-facing UI components
  - All interaction via REST API or admin

### R-053: No Onboarding Wizard
- **Description:** No setup wizard or multi-step onboarding
- **Source:** PRD §8; Decision 5
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - Admin screen is immediate; no "Get Started" flow
  - Setup instructions in README only

---

## Settings & Storage

### R-054: Settings Option Structure
- **Description:** All settings stored in `agentpress_settings` serialized option
- **Source:** PRD §7
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - Option key: `agentpress_settings`
  - Contains: `claude_api_key`, `cf_worker_url`, `default_model`, `installed_version`
  - Serialized as PHP array

### R-055: No Encrypted Storage
- **Description:** API keys NOT encrypted (security theater removed)
- **Source:** Decision 6
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - No encryption/decryption functions in codebase
  - Keys readable in database as plain text
  - README discloses plain-text storage

### R-056: wp-config.php Constant Support
- **Description:** Settings can be overridden via wp-config.php constants
- **Source:** Decision 6
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - Constants checked before option values (e.g., `AGENTPRESS_CLAUDE_KEY`)
  - If constant defined, UI field is disabled/hidden
  - Constants take precedence over saved options

---

## Distribution & Documentation

### R-057: WordPress.org Readme
- **Description:** `readme.txt` with three paragraphs; demo-focused
- **Source:** PRD §9; Decision 10
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - File `readme.txt` exists with WP.org header format
  - Description is ~3 paragraphs
  - Tags include: `ai`, `agents`, `automation`, `claude`, `orchestration`
  - API usage disclosed (per WordPress.org TOS)

### R-058: README Installation Instructions
- **Description:** README includes installation and cURL API example
- **Source:** PRD §9
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - Installation steps documented
  - cURL example for `/wp-json/agentpress/v1/run`
  - No third-party registration example (v2 feature)

### R-059: Plugin Assets for Repo
- **Description:** Banner (772×250) and screenshots captured
- **Source:** PRD §10 (Dogfooding)
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - Banner image exists at 772×250
  - At least one screenshot of admin dashboard
  - Assets ready for WP.org submission

### R-060: No Billing/SaaS Mentions
- **Description:** No "Pro", billing, or SaaS tier references in v1
- **Source:** Decision 8; PRD §3 (Non-Goals)
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - Codebase contains no "Pro", "Premium", "tier" references
  - README does not mention monetization
  - No upgrade prompts in admin UI

---

## Non-Functional Requirements

### R-061: No Async Queues
- **Description:** All processing is synchronous; no background queues
- **Source:** Decision 11; PRD §3 (Non-Goals)
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - No WP-Cron integration for task processing
  - No queue library or abstraction
  - All tasks complete within single request

### R-062: No BYOK Model
- **Description:** Single site-level API key; no per-user keys
- **Source:** Decision 11; PRD §14 (Open Questions)
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - Only one Claude API key configured per site
  - No user-level key assignment UI
  - BYOK noted as v2 trigger (500 daily tasks)

### R-063: 30-Second Timeout
- **Description:** REST endpoint times out at 30 seconds
- **Source:** PRD §14 (Open Questions)
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - `wp_remote_post` timeout set to 30s
  - Timeout returns structured error (not PHP fatal)
  - Error indicates timeout condition

### R-064: Latency Target < 4 Seconds
- **Description:** Average end-to-end latency under 4 seconds for built-in agents
- **Source:** PRD §13 (Success Metrics)
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - `latency_ms` in response averages < 4000ms
  - Measured over 100+ tasks
  - Local keyword routing should improve baseline

### R-065: Zero Fatal Errors
- **Description:** No fatal PHP errors under normal operation
- **Source:** PRD §13 (Success Metrics)
- **Acceptance Criteria:**
  - `wp_debug.log` shows no fatal errors after 100 tasks
  - All errors handled gracefully with WP_Error or REST error response

---

## Exclusions (Explicitly NOT in v1)

### R-066: Exclusion — SEOMeta Agent
- **Description:** SEOMeta agent is dead; does not ship
- **Source:** Decision 4
- **Acceptance Criteria:** No SEOMeta code, routes, or references

### R-067: Exclusion — Manual Task Runner UI
- **Description:** No textarea + Run button in admin
- **Source:** Decision 5
- **Acceptance Criteria:** No task input form in admin

### R-068: Exclusion — Developer API
- **Description:** No `agentpress_register_capability()` function
- **Source:** Decision 8
- **Acceptance Criteria:** No third-party registration mechanism

### R-069: Exclusion — Real-Time Streaming
- **Description:** No SSE, WebSocket, or chunked responses
- **Source:** Decision 11
- **Acceptance Criteria:** All responses are complete JSON

### R-070: Exclusion — Async Queues
- **Description:** No background job processing
- **Source:** Decision 11
- **Acceptance Criteria:** All tasks synchronous

### R-071: Exclusion — BYOK
- **Description:** No bring-your-own-key model
- **Source:** Decision 11
- **Acceptance Criteria:** Single site-level key only

### R-072: Exclusion — Front-End Chat
- **Description:** No chat widget or public UI
- **Source:** Decision 9
- **Acceptance Criteria:** REST API is only interface

### R-073: Exclusion — Onboarding Wizard
- **Description:** No setup wizard
- **Source:** Decision 5; PRD §8
- **Acceptance Criteria:** Single admin screen only

### R-074: Exclusion — Encrypted Key Storage
- **Description:** No wp_hash/base64 encryption
- **Source:** Decision 6
- **Acceptance Criteria:** Keys stored plain

### R-075: Exclusion — Third Agent (Functional)
- **Description:** Third agent slot reserved but not functional
- **Source:** Decision 4
- **Acceptance Criteria:** Placeholder file only; no registration

---

## Summary Count

| Category | Requirements |
|----------|-------------|
| Plugin Bootstrap & Structure | 4 |
| REST API Endpoint | 9 |
| Router & Routing Logic | 5 |
| JSON Parser Hardening | 5 |
| Built-In Agents | 13 |
| Capability Registry | 3 |
| Activity Logging | 4 |
| Admin Dashboard | 10 |
| Settings & Storage | 3 |
| Distribution & Documentation | 4 |
| Non-Functional | 5 |
| Exclusions | 10 |
| **Total** | **75** |

---

**Verification Note:** Each requirement above is atomic and independently verifiable. Build should proceed by checking off requirements as implemented. Any deviation from this spec requires decision log amendment.

# AgentPress v1 — Atomic Requirements

**Generated**: 2026-05-03
**Source PRD**: `/home/agent/shipyard-ai/prds/prd-agentpress-2026-05-03.md`
**Source Decisions**: `/home/agent/shipyard-ai/rounds/prd-agentpress-2026-05-03/decisions.md`
**Status**: Locked for Build Phase

> **Important:** `decisions.md` OVERRIDES the PRD where they conflict. The locked decisions (e.g., SEOMeta killed, manual runner killed, standard key storage, two agents only) are the binding spec.

---

## Plugin Bootstrap & Structure

| ID | Requirement | Source | Acceptance Criteria |
|----|-------------|--------|---------------------|
| R-001 | Plugin main file bootstrap | PRD §5.1, Decisions File Structure | `agentpress.php` exists with valid WP header, namespace `AgentPress`, activates without errors |
| R-002 | Activation — Capability CPT | PRD §5.1, Decision 7 | CPT `agentpress_capability` registered with `public => false`, `show_ui => false`, `supports => []` |
| R-003 | Activation — Log CPT | PRD §5.1, Decision 7 | CPT `agentpress_log` registered with `public => false`, `show_ui => false`, no revisions, no autosave |
| R-004 | File structure compliance | Decisions File Structure | All locked files exist; no extra files created. Third agent file is placeholder only |

---

## REST API Endpoint

| ID | Requirement | Source | Acceptance Criteria |
|----|-------------|--------|---------------------|
| R-005 | REST namespace | PRD §5.1, §6 | `agentpress/v1` registered; 404 on unregistered routes |
| R-006 | Single endpoint | PRD §5.3, Decision 5 | `POST /wp-json/agentpress/v1/run` accepts POST only; no other endpoints in v1 |
| R-007 | Auth — Nonce | PRD §5.3 | `X-WP-Nonce` from logged-in users accepted; invalid returns 401 |
| R-008 | Auth — Application Passwords | PRD §5.3 | Basic Auth with Application Password accepted; invalid returns 401 |
| R-009 | Authorization | PRD §5.3 | Requires `manage_options` or custom `agentpress_run` capability; 403 without |
| R-010 | Request validation | PRD §5.3, §6 | Missing `task` returns 400 `agentpress_missing_task`; optional `context` defaults to `{}` |
| R-011 | Success response shape | PRD §5.3, §6 | JSON contains `success` (bool), `routing` (object with `capability`, `reasoning`), `result` (object), `latency_ms` (int) |
| R-012 | Error response shape | PRD §6 | 400/500 errors include `code`, `message`, `data.status`; 500 includes `data.capability` |
| R-013 | Synchronous only | PRD §3, Decision 11 | Endpoint blocks until complete; no streaming; 30s timeout |

---

## Router & Routing Logic

| ID | Requirement | Source | Acceptance Criteria |
|----|-------------|--------|---------------------|
| R-014 | Local PHP keyword map | Decision 2 | `str_contains` matching routes built-in agents without LLM call |
| R-015 | Claude fallback | Decision 2 | Claude called ONLY when local map misses; fallback latency 4–8s acceptable |
| R-016 | Keyword whitelist locked | Decisions Open Q #2 | Content keywords (`write`, `content`, `blog`, `post`, `article`, `text`) → `content_writer`; image keywords (`image`, `picture`, `photo`, `graphic`) → `image_generator` |
| R-017 | System prompt construction | PRD §5.3 | Prompt includes capability manifest; instructs Claude to return ONLY JSON (no markdown); specifies schema `{"capability":"...","reasoning":"...","payload":{...}}` |
| R-018 | No third-party routing | Decision 8 | Router handles only built-in agents; no external capability calls in v1 |

---

## JSON Parser Hardening (Non-Negotiable)

| ID | Requirement | Source | Acceptance Criteria |
|----|-------------|--------|---------------------|
| R-019 | Markdown fence stripping | Decision 12 | ` ```json...``` ` responses parsed correctly; fences removed before decode |
| R-020 | Truncated response handling | Decision 12 | Partial JSON does not fatal-error; returns structured parser error |
| R-021 | Hallucinated slug handling | Decision 12 | Unknown slugs return error (not crash); fallback to `capability: none` |
| R-022 | Schema validation | Decisions Open Q #7 | Required field `capability` validated; missing/invalid fields rejected with error |
| R-023 | Parser budget honored | Decision 12 | Dedicated `class-parser.php` exists; tested with markdown fences, truncated JSON, hallucinated keys before any other feature |

---

## Built-In Agents

| ID | Requirement | Source | Acceptance Criteria |
|----|-------------|--------|---------------------|
| R-024 | ContentWriter registration | PRD §5.4, Decision 4 | Slug `content_writer`; callable via router; in capability list |
| R-025 | ContentWriter input schema | PRD §5.4 | Accepts `topic` (string), `tone` (enum: friendly/professional/witty), `length` (enum: short/medium/long); invalid values return error |
| R-026 | ContentWriter Claude integration | PRD §5.4 | Calls Anthropic Messages API via `wp_remote_post` with configured key; handles API errors gracefully |
| R-027 | ContentWriter guardrail | PRD §5.4 | Output truncated to 2048 chars max; `tokens_used` still reported |
| R-028 | ContentWriter output shape | PRD §5.4 | Returns `{"text":"...","tokens_used":42}` |
| R-029 | ImageGenerator registration | PRD §5.4, Decision 4 | Slug `image_generator`; callable via router; in capability list |
| R-030 | ImageGenerator input schema | PRD §5.4 | Accepts `prompt` (string), `size` (enum: 512x512/1024x1024); invalid values return error |
| R-031 | ImageGenerator CF Workers integration | PRD §5.4 | Calls configured `cf_worker_url` via `wp_remote_post`; handles Worker errors gracefully |
| R-032 | ImageGenerator HTTPS guardrail | PRD §5.4 | Returned URL must be HTTPS; HTTP rejected |
| R-033 | ImageGenerator output shape | PRD §5.4 | Returns `{"url":"https://...","format":"webp","size":"1024x1024"}` |
| R-034 | ImageGenerator error shape | PRD §5.4 | Failed Worker call returns structured WP_Error-style error with original message |
| R-035 | SEOMeta excluded | Decision 4 | No `seo_meta` capability, no `class-seo-meta.php`, no references in code |
| R-036 | Third agent placeholder | Decision 4, File Structure | File `includes/agents/class-agent-third.php` exists as minimal stub; not registered |

---

## Capability Registry

| ID | Requirement | Source | Acceptance Criteria |
|----|-------------|--------|---------------------|
| R-037 | CPT-based storage | PRD §5.2, Decision 7 | `agentpress_capability` CPT stores `_agentpress_name`, `_agentpress_description`, `_agentpress_input_schema`, `_agentpress_handler` as post_meta; `post_name` = slug |
| R-038 | Built-in auto-registration | PRD §5.2, Decision 4 | ContentWriter and ImageGenerator inserted into CPT on activation; immediately queryable |
| R-039 | No third-party API | Decision 8 | `agentpress_register_capability()` function does NOT exist in v1; no external registration mechanism |

---

## Activity Logging

| ID | Requirement | Source | Acceptance Criteria |
|----|-------------|--------|---------------------|
| R-040 | Log every task | PRD §5.3, Decision 7 | Log entry created on every task completion (success or error); `post_title` = truncated task string |
| R-041 | Log meta fields | PRD §5.3, §7, Decisions Open Q #4 | Meta: `_agentpress_capability`, `_agentpress_payload`, `_agentpress_result`, `_agentpress_latency_ms`, `_agentpress_status` (`success` or `error`); timestamp = `post_date` |
| R-042 | Auto-prune | PRD §7, Decisions Open Q #4 | On insert, if count > 500, delete oldest 50; pruning non-blocking; count never exceeds 550 |
| R-043 | Log viewer | PRD §5.5, Decision 7 | Admin displays last 50 logs with time, capability, status pill (green/red), latency |

---

## Admin Dashboard

| ID | Requirement | Source | Acceptance Criteria |
|----|-------------|--------|---------------------|
| R-044 | Single screen, no tabs | Decision 3, Decision 5 | Page slug `tools-agentpress` under Tools menu; no tab navigation; no wizards |
| R-045 | No manual task runner | Decision 5 | No textarea + Run button; testing via cURL/Postman only |
| R-046 | Claude API key field | PRD §5.5, Decision 6 | Text input (type="password"), saved to `agentpress_settings`; supports `AGENTPRESS_CLAUDE_KEY` constant override |
| R-047 | Cloudflare Worker URL field | PRD §5.5 | Text input for Worker URL, saved to `agentpress_settings`; URL format validation |
| R-048 | Default model select | PRD §5.5 | Dropdown with `claude-3-5-sonnet-20241022` as default; saved to `agentpress_settings` |
| R-049 | Standard key storage | Decision 6 | Plain option storage; no `wp_hash`/`base64_encode`; `wp-config.php` constants take precedence; UI disabled when constant is defined |
| R-050 | Native WordPress styling | PRD §5.5, §8, Decision 3 | Uses `.wp-list-table`, `.form-table`; no CSS frameworks; custom CSS minimal (spacing/typography only); WP admin colors |
| R-051 | Activity log display | PRD §5.5 | Table shows timestamp, capability, status pill, latency; most recent first |
| R-052 | No front-end chat | Decision 9 | No shortcode, no public UI; REST API only interface |
| R-053 | No onboarding wizard | PRD §8, Decision 5 | Single admin screen; setup in README only |

---

## Settings & Storage

| ID | Requirement | Source | Acceptance Criteria |
|----|-------------|--------|---------------------|
| R-054 | Settings option structure | PRD §7 | Option key `agentpress_settings`; serialized array with `claude_api_key`, `cf_worker_url`, `default_model`, `installed_version` |
| R-055 | No encrypted storage | Decision 6 | No encryption/decryption functions; keys readable as plain text in DB |
| R-056 | wp-config constant support | Decision 6 | Constants `AGENTPRESS_CLAUDE_KEY`, `AGENTPRESS_CF_WORKER_URL`, `AGENTPRESS_DEFAULT_MODEL` checked before option values; UI fields disabled when constants defined |

---

## Distribution & Documentation

| ID | Requirement | Source | Acceptance Criteria |
|----|-------------|--------|---------------------|
| R-057 | readme.txt | PRD §9, Decision 10 | WP.org header format; ~3 paragraphs; tags: `ai`, `agents`, `automation`, `claude`, `orchestration`; API usage disclosed |
| R-058 | README API example | PRD §9 | cURL example for `POST /wp-json/agentpress/v1/run`; installation steps |
| R-059 | No billing/SaaS mentions | Decision 8 | No "Pro", "Premium", "tier" references; no monetization mentions |
| R-060 | No third-party registration docs | Decision 8 | README does not document `agentpress_register_capability()`; v2 only |

---

## Non-Functional & Exclusions

| ID | Requirement | Source | Acceptance Criteria |
|----|-------------|--------|---------------------|
| R-061 | No async queues | Decision 11 | No WP-Cron task processing; no queue library; all tasks synchronous |
| R-062 | No BYOK | Decision 11 | Single site-level key; no per-user keys; BYOK noted as v2 trigger |
| R-063 | 30-second timeout | PRD §14 | `wp_remote_post` timeout = 30s; timeout returns structured error |
| R-064 | Latency target | PRD §13 | Average `latency_ms` < 4000 for built-in agents (measured over 100+ tasks) |
| R-065 | Zero fatal errors | PRD §13 | No fatal PHP errors after 100 tasks; all errors handled gracefully |
| R-066-R-075 | Exclusions verified | Decisions 4–9, 11 | SEOMeta dead, manual runner dead, no dev API, no streaming, no async, no BYOK, no front-end chat, no onboarding, no encrypted storage, third agent placeholder only |

---

## Requirements Summary

| Category | Count |
|----------|-------|
| Plugin Bootstrap | 4 |
| REST API | 9 |
| Router | 5 |
| Parser | 5 |
| Built-In Agents | 13 |
| Capability Registry | 3 |
| Activity Logging | 4 |
| Admin Dashboard | 10 |
| Settings & Storage | 3 |
| Distribution | 4 |
| Non-Functional | 15 |
| **Total** | **71** |

---

## Risk Notes (from Hindsight + Risk Scanner)

**Hindsight Context**: The AgentPress plugin is a **net-new codebase** with no direct overlap with high-churn or bug-associated files from the Shipyard AI repo. Uncommitted changes exist in `.agent-logs/debate/`, `prds/`, and `rounds/` — these are debate artifacts for this PRD and do not affect the build.

**High-Risk Files** (flagged for careful sequencing):
1. `includes/class-parser.php` — Single point of failure for entire pipeline. JSON edge cases (markdown fences, truncated responses, hallucinated slugs) will sink the product if not hardened.
2. `includes/class-router.php` — Keyword map must correctly route; Claude fallback adds 4–8s latency. Wrong routing = wrong agent = broken UX.
3. `includes/class-logger.php` — CPT writes per task. Shared hosting (64MB, cheap DB) can choke. No revisions, no autosave, aggressive pruning required.
4. `includes/agents/class-image-generator.php` — Image generation timeouts on shared hosts with 30s PHP limit. Fallback strategy needed.
5. `includes/class-rest-api.php` — All errors and success flow through here. Error shape must be consistent and informative.

**Mitigation Strategy**:
- Build parser FIRST in Wave 1 and test with 10+ edge cases before any agent logic.
- Build router in Wave 3 only after agents are proven working.
- REST API in Wave 4 ties everything together.
- Admin UI in Wave 5 — after all backend behavior is locked.

---

### Wave 1 — Foundation (Parallel)
*Build the plugin skeleton, parser, and minimal admin CSS. These tasks have no interdependencies.*

### Wave 3 — Orchestration (after Wave 2)
*The router ties the keyword map and Claude fallback together. It depends on the agents being registered and the parser being hardened.*

<task-plan id="phase-1-task-8" wave="3">
  <title>Router — Keyword Map + Claude Fallback</title>
  <requirement>R-014, R-015, R-016, R-017, R-018</requirement>
  <description>
    Build the orchestration layer. Local PHP keyword map short-circuits obvious intents (write/blog/post → ContentWriter; image/photo/picture → ImageGenerator). Claude fallback handles ambiguous input. This is the product's soul — "one mind, not a bag of tools."
  </description>

  <context>
    <file path="/home/agent/shipyard-ai/prds/prd-agentpress-2026-05-03.md" reason="PRD §5.3 (router flow, system prompt, Claude integration)" />
    <file path="/home/agent/shipyard-ai/rounds/prd-agentpress-2026-05-03/decisions.md" reason="Decision 2 (router architecture stays: local keyword map + Claude fallback); Decisions Open Q #2 (keyword whitelist needs locking)" />
    <file path="/home/agent/shipyard-ai/.planning/REQUIREMENTS.md" reason="R-014 through R-018 acceptance criteria" />
    <file path="/home/agent/shipyard-ai/deliverables/relay-ai-form-handler/includes/class-claude-client.php" reason="Reusable Claude API client pattern for Messages API" />
  </context>

  <steps>
    <step order="1">Create `includes/class-router.php` with namespace `AgentPress`. Class `Router`.</step>
    <step order="2">Define locked keyword whitelist in code as associative array:
      - ContentWriter triggers: `['write', 'content', 'blog', 'post', 'article', 'text', 'draft', 'compose', 'generate text']`
      - ImageGenerator triggers: `['image', 'picture', 'photo', 'graphic', 'generate image', 'create image', 'featured image']`
    </step>
    <step order="3">Implement `Router::route_local($task)` that checks `$task` string (case-insensitive) against keyword map using `stripos`. Returns matched capability slug or `null`.</step>
    <step order="4">Implement `Router::route_claude($task, $context)` that builds system prompt with capability manifest from `Agents::build_manifest()`. Prompt instructs Claude to return ONLY JSON: `{"capability":"slug","reasoning":"...","payload":{...}}` or `{"capability":"none","reasoning":"..."}`.</step>
    <step order="5">Call Claude Messages API via `wp_remote_post` with timeout 30s. Parse response with `Parser::extract_json()` and `Parser::validate_routing_json()`.</step>
    <step order="6">If Claude returns `capability: none` or unknown slug, return WP_Error with `agentpress_no_match` code.</step>
    <step order="7">Implement `Router::route($task, $context)` that tries local map first; if miss, calls Claude fallback; logs routing decision for debugging.</step>
    <step order="8">Return routing result array: `['capability' => $slug, 'reasoning' => $reason, 'payload' => $payload, 'source' => 'local'|'claude']`.</step>
  </steps>

### Wave 4 — Integration (after Wave 3)
*The REST API endpoint wires auth, validation, router, agents, parser, and logger into a single synchronous interface. This is the only public API surface.*

<task-plan id="phase-1-task-9" wave="4">
  <title>REST API Endpoint — Single Public Interface</title>
  <requirement>R-005, R-006, R-007, R-008, R-009, R-010, R-011, R-012, R-013, R-063</requirement>
  <description>
    Build the single REST endpoint `POST /wp-json/agentpress/v1/run`. It authenticates, validates, routes, executes, logs, and returns the standardized response shape. No other endpoints. No streaming. Synchronous only. This is the primary interface — the only way the world talks to AgentPress.
  </description>

  <context>
    <file path="/home/agent/shipyard-ai/prds/prd-agentpress-2026-05-03.md" reason="PRD §5.3 (full flow), §6 (API spec, request/response shapes)" />
    <file path="/home/agent/shipyard-ai/rounds/prd-agentpress-2026-05-03/decisions.md" reason="Decision 5 (REST endpoint is the only interface); Decision 9 (no front-end chat); Decision 11 (no streaming, synchronous only)" />
    <file path="/home/agent/shipyard-ai/.planning/REQUIREMENTS.md" reason="R-005 through R-013, R-063 acceptance criteria" />
    <file path="/home/agent/shipyard-ai/deliverables/prd-agentbridge-wordpress-mcp-server/build/agentbridge/includes/class-server.php" reason="Reusable REST route registration, permission callback, JSON response pattern" />
  </context>

  <steps>
    <step order="1">Create `includes/class-rest-api.php` with namespace `AgentPress`. Class `REST_API`.</step>
    <step order="2">Implement `REST_API::register_routes()` hooked to `rest_api_init`. Register `POST /agentpress/v1/run` with:
      - `permission_callback`: check `current_user_can('manage_options')` OR `current_user_can('agentpress_run')`.
      - `callback`: `REST_API::run_task()`.
      - `args`: validate `task` is required string, `context` is optional object.
    </step>
    <step order="3">In `run_task($request)`: capture `microtime(true)` as `$start_time`. Extract `task` and `context` from request body.</step>
    <step order="4">If `task` is empty/missing, return `WP_REST_Response` with 400 and code `agentpress_missing_task`, message "Task string is required."</step>
    <step order="5">Call `Router::route($task, $context)` to determine capability and payload.</step>
    <step order="6">If routing returns WP_Error, log the error via `Logger::log()`, calculate latency, and return 500 with code `agentpress_agent_error`, message from error, data includes `capability` if known.</step>
    <step order="7">If routing succeeds, call `Agents::run_internal($capability, $payload)` to execute the agent.</step>
    <step order="8">If agent execution returns WP_Error, log via `Logger::log()`, calculate latency, return 500 with code `agentpress_agent_error`, message, data includes `capability`.</step>
    <step order="9">If execution succeeds, log via `Logger::log()` with status `success`, calculate `$latency_ms = intval((microtime(true) - $start_time) * 1000)`.</step>
    <step order="10">Return 200 JSON response: `{'success' => true, 'routing' => ['capability' => $slug, 'reasoning' => $reason], 'result' => $result, 'latency_ms' => $latency_ms}`.</step>
    <step order="11">Ensure response headers include `Content-Type: application/json`. No caching headers.</step>

## Wave Summary

| Wave | Tasks | Theme | Parallel? |
|------|-------|-------|-----------|
| 1 | task-1, task-2, task-3 | Foundation | Yes |
| 2 | task-4, task-5, task-6, task-7 | Core Classes | Yes |
| 3 | task-8 | Orchestration | Sequential |
| 4 | task-9 | Integration | Sequential |
| 5 | task-10, task-11 | Admin & Polish | Yes |

---

## Risk Register (Build Phase)

| Risk | Likelihood | Impact | Mitigation in Plan |
|------|------------|--------|-------------------|
| JSON parser collapses pipeline | High | Critical | Built in Wave 1 with 10+ edge-case tests before any agent logic |
| Image generation timeout on shared host | Medium | Medium | Timeout guard in agent, user-friendly error message, URL return only |
| CPT logging bloats DB | Medium | High | Auto-prune on every insert, no revisions, no autosave, force delete |
| Admin UI ugly-premium gap | Medium | Medium | CSS in Wave 1 is minimal; admin reviewed against native WP patterns |
| Keyword map misses intent | Medium | High | Claude fallback always available; conservative whitelist with room to expand |
| Third agent scope creep | Medium | Medium | Placeholder stub only; exclusion audit in task-11 catches any drift |
| WordPress.org rejection | Low | High | API disclosure in readme.txt; no encrypted storage theater |

---

## Open Questions Resolution (Before Code)

The following open questions from `decisions.md` are resolved by this plan:

1. **Third agent identity** → Reserved slot stays empty. `class-agent-third.php` is a placeholder stub (task-11, step 7).
2. **Local keyword map exact scope** → Locked whitelist defined in task-8, step 2. Content vs Image keywords explicitly enumerated.
3. **Admin page visual language** → Native WP `form-table` + `wp-list-table` elevated with 100-line CSS (task-3). No tabs, no custom frameworks.
4. **Activity log CPT schema** → Meta fields: `_agentpress_capability`, `_agentpress_payload`, `_agentpress_result`, `_agentpress_latency_ms`, `_agentpress_status`. Prune: delete oldest 50 when count > 500 (task-5).
5. **Image handling flow** → Return HTTPS URL only. Store in Media Library is NOT in v1; URL return avoids shared-host file limits (task-7, step 6).
6. **Parser hardening strategy** → Layered approach: `json_decode()` → regex strip fences → regex extract first `{...}` → WP_Error fallback (task-2, step 2).
7. **Error surfaced to user vs logged silently** → REST endpoint returns structured JSON errors (400/500) with `code`, `message`, `data`. All errors also logged to CPT (task-9, steps 6, 8, 10).

---

   384→  <description>
   385→    Build the single REST endpoint `POST /wp-json/agentpress/v1/run`. It authenticates, validates, routes, executes, logs, and returns the standardized response shape. No other endpoints. No streaming. Synchronous only. This is the primary interface — the only way the world talks to AgentPress.

   397→    <step order="2">Implement `REST_API::register_routes()` hooked to `rest_api_init`. Register `POST /agentpress/v1/run` with:
   398→      - `permission_callback`: check `current_user_can('manage_options')` OR `current_user_can('agentpress_run')`.
   399→      - `callback`: `REST_API::run_task()`.

   404→    <step order="5">Call `Router::route($task, $context)` to determine capability and payload.</step>
   405→    <step order="6">If routing returns WP_Error, log the error via `Logger::log()`, calculate latency, and return 500 with code `agentpress_agent_error`, message from error, data includes `capability` if known.</step>

**Requirements**: `/home/agent/shipyard-ai/.planning/REQUIREMENTS.md`

**Waves**: 5
**Build Target**: Single session, shippable MVP

---

## Requirements Traceability

| Requirement | Task(s) | Wave |
|-------------|---------|------|
| R-001, R-002, R-003, R-004, R-038, R-056 | phase-1-task-1 | 1 |

| R-037, R-039 | phase-1-task-4 | 2 |

| R-005, R-006, R-007, R-008, R-009, R-010, R-011, R-012, R-013, R-063 | phase-1-task-9 | 4 |
| R-044, R-045, R-046, R-047, R-048, R-049, R-050, R-051, R-052, R-053 | phase-1-task-10 | 5 |
| R-057, R-058, R-059, R-060, R-061, R-062, R-064, R-065, R-066–R-075 | phase-1-task-11 | 5 |

---

## Risk Notes (from Hindsight + Risk Scanner)

**Hindsight Context**: The AgentPress plugin is a **net-new codebase** with no direct overlap with high-churn or bug-associated files from the Shipyard AI repo. Uncommitted changes exist in `.agent-logs/debate/`, `prds/`, and `rounds/` — these are debate artifacts for this PRD and do not affect the build.

**High-Risk Files** (flagged for careful sequencing):
1. `includes/class-parser.php` — Single point of failure for entire pipeline. JSON edge cases (markdown fences, truncated responses, hallucinated slugs) will sink the product if not hardened.
2. `includes/class-router.php` — Keyword map must correctly route; Claude fallback adds 4–8s latency. Wrong routing = wrong agent = broken UX.
3. `includes/class-logger.php` — CPT writes per task. Shared hosting (64MB, cheap DB) can choke. No revisions, no autosave, aggressive pruning required.
4. `includes/agents/class-image-generator.php` — Image generation timeouts on shared hosts with 30s PHP limit. Fallback strategy needed.
5. `includes/class-rest-api.php` — All errors and success flow through here. Error shape must be consistent and informative.

**Mitigation Strategy**:
- Build parser FIRST in Wave 1 and test with 10+ edge cases before any agent logic.
- Build router in Wave 3 only after agents are proven working.
- REST API in Wave 4 ties everything together.
- Admin UI in Wave 5 — after all backend behavior is locked.

---

## Wave Execution Order

### Wave 1 — Foundation (Parallel)
*Build the plugin skeleton, parser, and minimal admin CSS. These tasks have no interdependencies.*

  <title>Plugin Bootstrap + CPT Registration</title>
  <requirement>R-001, R-002, R-003, R-004, R-038, R-056</requirement>

Auto-register ContentWriter and ImageGenerator capability posts on first activation. Set up the default settings option with wp-config.php constant fallback support.
  </description>

  <context>
    <file path="/home/agent/shipyard-ai/prds/prd-agentpress-2026-05-03.md" reason="PRD §5.1 (bootstrap), §5.2 (registry), §7 (CPT schema, option schema)" />

    <file path="/home/agent/shipyard-ai/.planning/REQUIREMENTS.md" reason="R-001 through R-004, R-038, R-056 acceptance criteria" />
    <file path="/home/agent/shipyard-ai/deliverables/adminpulse/adminpulse.php" reason="Reusable plugin header + activation hook pattern" />
    <file path="/home/agent/shipyard-ai/deliverables/wp-intelligence-suite/wis-core/wis-core.php" reason="Default options with existence checks pattern" />
  </context>

  <steps>
    <step order="1">Create `agentpress/agentpress.php` with standard WP plugin header (Plugin Name: AgentPress, Version: 1.0.0, Author: Shipyard AI, Text Domain: agentpress). Define namespace `AgentPress`.</step>
    <step order="2">Define plugin constants: `AGENTPRESS_VERSION`, `AGENTPRESS_PLUGIN_DIR`, `AGENTPRESS_PLUGIN_URL`.</step>
    <step order="3">Write activation hook that registers CPT `agentpress_capability` with `public => false`, `show_ui => false`, `show_in_menu => false`, `supports => array('title')`, `rewrite => false`, `has_archive => false`.</step>
    <step order="4">Write activation hook that registers CPT `agentpress_log` with `public => false`, `show_ui => false`, `show_in_menu => false`, `supports => array()`, `rewrite => false`, `has_archive => false`. Explicitly disable revisions and autosave via filters.</step>
    <step order="5">On activation, insert two `agentpress_capability` posts: slug `content_writer` (title "Content Writer") and slug `image_generator` (title "Image Generator"). Store post_meta: `_agentpress_name`, `_agentpress_description`, `_agentpress_input_schema` (serialized array per PRD §5.4), `_agentpress_handler` = `internal`.</step>
    <step order="6">On activation, create default option `agentpress_settings` if it does not exist: `array('claude_api_key' => '', 'cf_worker_url' => '', 'default_model' => 'claude-3-5-sonnet-20241022', 'installed_version' => '1.0.0')`.</step>
    <step order="7">Implement `agentpress_get_setting($key)` helper that checks `wp-config.php` constants first (`AGENTPRESS_CLAUDE_KEY`, `AGENTPRESS_CF_WORKER_URL`, `AGENTPRESS_DEFAULT_MODEL`) before falling back to the option value.</step>

<task-plan id="phase-1-task-2" wave="1">
  <title>JSON Parser Hardening</title>
  <requirement>R-019, R-020, R-021, R-022, R-023</requirement>
  <description>
    Build the JSON parser utility that extracts and validates JSON from Claude responses. This is the highest-risk file in the entire product. It must handle markdown fences, truncated JSON, hallucinated slugs, and malformed responses without crashing the pipeline. Budget 30 minutes of build time for edge-case testing.
  </description>

  <context>
    <file path="/home/agent/shipyard-ai/prds/prd-agentpress-2026-05-03.md" reason="PRD §5.3 (parse JSON from Claude response), §6 (error shapes)" />
    <file path="/home/agent/shipyard-ai/rounds/prd-agentpress-2026-05-03/decisions.md" reason="Decision 12 (JSON parser hardening is non-negotiable); Risk Register (JSON parsing collapses pipeline = High/Critical)" />
    <file path="/home/agent/shipyard-ai/.planning/REQUIREMENTS.md" reason="R-019 through R-023 acceptance criteria" />

  </context>

  <steps>
    <step order="1">Create `includes/class-parser.php` with namespace `AgentPress`. Class `Parser` with static methods.</step>
    <step order="2">Implement `Parser::extract_json($raw_response)` with layered extraction: (1) try `json_decode()` directly with `JSON_THROW_ON_ERROR` in try/catch; (2) if that fails, regex strip markdown code fences (`/^```json\s*|\s*```$/m`); (3) if still fails, regex extract first `{...}` block using recursive brace matching; (4) if all fail, return WP_Error with `agentpress_parser_error`.</step>
    <step order="3">Implement `Parser::validate_routing_json($data)` that checks: `$data` is array, `capability` key exists and is string, `reasoning` key exists and is string (optional but recommended), `payload` key exists and is array/object (optional). Return WP_Error with specific message if validation fails.</step>
    <step order="4">Implement `Parser::sanitize_payload($payload)` that recursively walks arrays and applies `wp_kses_post` to all string values.</step>
    <step order="5">Create a test harness file `tests/parser-test.php` (outside plugin loader) with 10+ test cases: valid JSON, markdown-fenced JSON, truncated JSON, missing capability key, hallucinated slug (`{"capability":"seo_meta"}`), nested payload, empty response, HTML-wrapped JSON, extra text before/after JSON, invalid JSON syntax.</step>
    <step order="6">Run all test cases manually (CLI or browser) and verify each returns expected result (parsed array or WP_Error). Fix any failures before proceeding.</step>
  </steps>

    <step order="7">Return array: `['text' => $text, 'tokens_used => $tokens_used]`.</step>
